민수기 33:1.content: These are the journeys of the children of Israel, when they went out of the land of Egypt by their armies under the hand of Moses and Aaron.

민수기 33:2.content: Moses wrote the starting points of their journeys by the commandment of Yahweh. These are their journeys according to their starting points.

민수기 33:3.content: They traveled from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the next day after the Passover, the children of Israel went out with a high hand in the sight of all the Egyptians,

민수기 33:4.content: while the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn, whom Yahweh had struck among them. Yahweh also executed judgments on their gods.

민수기 33:5.content: The children of Israel traveled from Rameses, and encamped in Succoth.

민수기 33:6.content: They traveled from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, which is in the edge of the wilderness.

민수기 33:7.content: They traveled from Etham, and turned back to Pihahiroth, which is before Baal Zephon, and they encamped before Migdol.

민수기 33:8.content: They traveled from before Hahiroth, and crossed through the middle of the sea into the wilderness. They went three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham, and encamped in Marah.

민수기 33:9.content: They traveled from Marah, and came to Elim. In Elim, there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they encamped there.

민수기 33:10.content: They traveled from Elim, and encamped by the Red Sea.

민수기 33:11.content: They traveled from the Red Sea, and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.

민수기 33:12.content: They traveled from the wilderness of Sin, and encamped in Dophkah.

민수기 33:13.content: They traveled from Dophkah, and encamped in Alush.

민수기 33:14.content: They traveled from Alush, and encamped in Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink.

민수기 33:15.content: They traveled from Rephidim, and encamped in the wilderness of Sinai.

민수기 33:16.content: They traveled from the wilderness of Sinai, and encamped in Kibroth Hattaavah.

민수기 33:17.content: They traveled from Kibroth Hattaavah, and encamped in Hazeroth.

민수기 33:18.content: They traveled from Hazeroth, and encamped in Rithmah.

민수기 33:19.content: They traveled from Rithmah, and encamped in Rimmon Perez.

민수기 33:20.content: They traveled from Rimmon Perez, and encamped in Libnah.

민수기 33:21.content: They traveled from Libnah, and encamped in Rissah.

민수기 33:22.content: They traveled from Rissah, and encamped in Kehelathah.

민수기 33:23.content: They traveled from Kehelathah, and encamped in Mount Shepher.

민수기 33:24.content: They traveled from Mount Shepher, and encamped in Haradah.

민수기 33:25.content: They traveled from Haradah, and encamped in Makheloth.

민수기 33:26.content: They traveled from Makheloth, and encamped in Tahath.

민수기 33:27.content: They traveled from Tahath, and encamped in Terah.

민수기 33:28.content: They traveled from Terah, and encamped in Mithkah.

민수기 33:29.content: They traveled from Mithkah, and encamped in Hashmonah.

민수기 33:30.content: They traveled from Hashmonah, and encamped in Moseroth.

민수기 33:31.content: They traveled from Moseroth, and encamped in Bene Jaakan.

민수기 33:32.content: They traveled from Bene Jaakan, and encamped in Hor Haggidgad.

민수기 33:33.content: They traveled from Hor Haggidgad, and encamped in Jotbathah.

민수기 33:34.content: They traveled from Jotbathah, and encamped in Abronah.

민수기 33:35.content: They traveled from Abronah, and encamped in Ezion Geber.

민수기 33:36.content: They traveled from Ezion Geber, and encamped at Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.

민수기 33:37.content: They traveled from Kadesh, and encamped in Mount Hor, in the edge of the land of Edom.

민수기 33:38.content: Aaron the priest went up into Mount Hor at the commandment of Yahweh and died there, in the fortieth year after the children of Israel had come out of the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first day of the month.

민수기 33:39.content: Aaron was one hundred twenty-three years old when he died in Mount Hor.

민수기 33:40.content: The Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the South in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming of the children of Israel.

민수기 33:41.content: They traveled from Mount Hor, and encamped in Zalmonah.

민수기 33:42.content: They traveled from Zalmonah, and encamped in Punon.

민수기 33:43.content: They traveled from Punon, and encamped in Oboth.

민수기 33:44.content: They traveled from Oboth, and encamped in Iye Abarim, in the border of Moab.

민수기 33:45.content: They traveled from Iyim, and encamped in Dibon Gad.

민수기 33:46.content: They traveled from Dibon Gad, and encamped in Almon Diblathaim.

민수기 33:47.content: They traveled from Almon Diblathaim, and encamped in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.

민수기 33:48.content: They traveled from the mountains of Abarim, and encamped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.

민수기 33:49.content: They encamped by the Jordan, from Beth Jeshimoth even to Abel Shittim in the plains of Moab.

민수기 33:50.content: Yahweh spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,

민수기 33:51.content: Speak to the children of Israel, and tell them, “When you pass over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

민수기 33:52.content: then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, destroy all their stone idols, destroy all their molten images, and demolish all their high places.

민수기 33:53.content: You shall take possession of the land, and dwell therein; for I have given the land to you to possess it.

민수기 33:54.content: You shall inherit the land by lot according to your families; to the more you shall give the more inheritance, and to the fewer you shall give the less inheritance. Wherever the lot falls to any man, that shall be his. You shall inherit according to the tribes of your fathers.

민수기 33:55.content: “But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those you let remain of them will be like pricks in your eyes and thorns in your sides. They will harass you in the land in which you dwell.

민수기 33:56.content: It shall happen that as I thought to do to them, so I will do to you.”
